alternative_parents.description: Carboxylic acids containing exactly four carboxyl groups.
alternative_parents.name: Tetracarboxylic acids and derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Compounds consisting of an amine with a beta-N-glycosidic bond to a carbohydrate, thus forming a cyclic hemiaminal ether bond (alpha-amino ether).
alternative_parents.name: Glycosylamines

alternative_parents.description: Monosaccharides in which the carbohydrate moiety contains five carbon atoms.
alternative_parents.name: Pentoses

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing  a five-member aromatic ring of two carbon atoms and three nitrogen atoms.
alternative_parents.name: Triazoles

alternative_parents.description: Heterocyclic compounds containing a saturated, aliphatic, five-membered ring where a carbon is replaced by an oxygen.
alternative_parents.name: Tetrahydrofurans

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a carboxyl group that is esterified with a methyl group. They have the general structure RC(=O)OR', where R=H or organyl group and R'=methyl group.
alternative_parents.name: Methyl esters

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing an aromatic ring where a carbon atom is linked to an hetero atom.
alternative_parents.name: Heteroaromatic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an heterocycle with at least one oxygen atom linked to a ring carbon.
alternative_parents.name: Oxacyclic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an heterocycle with at least one nitrogen atom and one carbon atom linked to each other.
alternative_parents.name: Azacyclic compounds

alternative_parents.description: Compounds containing a bond between carbon a pnictogen atom. Pnictogens are p-block element atoms that are in the group 15 of the periodic table.
alternative_parents.name: Organopnictogen compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a nitrogen atom.
alternative_parents.name: Organonitrogen compounds

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing an oxide group.
alternative_parents.name: Organic oxides

alternative_parents.description: Derivatives of hydrocarbons obtained by substituting one or more carbon atoms by an heteroatom. They contain at least one carbon atom and heteroatom.
alternative_parents.name: Hydrocarbon derivatives

alternative_parents.description: Organic compounds containing a carbonyl group, with the general structure RC(=O)R', where R=organyl, R'=H, N, O, organyl group or halide group.
alternative_parents.name: Carbonyl compounds

class.description: Nucleoside derivatives containing a ribose (or deoxyribose) moiety which is N-glycosylated to a triazole.  Nucleotides have a phosphate group linked to the C5 carbon of the ribose (or deoxyribose) moiety.
class.name: Triazole ribonucleosides and ribonucleotides

description: This compound belongs to the class of organic compounds known as triazole ribonucleosides and ribonucleotides. These are nucleoside derivatives containing a ribose (or deoxyribose) moiety which is N-glycosylated to a triazole.  Nucleotides have a phosphate group linked to the C5 carbon of the ribose (or deoxyribose) moiety.

direct_parent.description: Nucleoside derivatives containing a ribose (or deoxyribose) moiety which is N-glycosylated to a triazole.  Nucleotides have a phosphate group linked to the C5 carbon of the ribose (or deoxyribose) moiety.
direct_parent.name: Triazole ribonucleosides and ribonucleotides

kingdom.description: Compounds that contain at least one carbon atom, excluding isocyanide/cyanide and their non-hydrocarbyl derivatives, thiophosgene, carbon diselenide, carbon monosulfide, carbon disulfide, carbon subsulfide, carbon monoxide, carbon dioxide, Carbon suboxide, and dicarbon monoxide.
kingdom.name: Organic compounds

superclass.description: Compounds containing a nucleobase linked to a ribose or deoxyribose sugar via a beta-glycosidic linkage. The ribose or deoxyribose moiety bears at least a phosphate group in case of nucleotides.
superclass.name: Nucleosides, nucleotides, and analogues
